svg.title: Fault response and hazard divert scenario comparison
svg.text: Identical initial state, estimator, corridor guidance, and finite-rate command path; only the stated fault or target changes.
prop 2938 kg | t_f 44.4 s | nav rejects 0
prop 3304 kg | t_f 39.6 s | nav rejects 0
prop 2950 kg | t_f 44.2 s | nav rejects 0
prop 2484 kg | t_f 50.5 s | nav rejects 435
prop 2942 kg | t_f 44.3 s | nav rejects 0
The green S-curve is a lateral impulse-and-brake sequence:
early tilt accelerates toward the safe site; opposite tilt later
removes crossrange velocity. Touchdown is 5.47 m clear.
An 8% loss remains inside the available impulse/time-to-go
margin. At 18% loss, flight ends 4.8 s earlier and e_x
The +12 m altitude step produces 435 gated innovations.
Estimator consistency is retained, but flight extends to 50.5 s,
adding gravity loss and using 454 kg vs nominal.
Interpretation: positive propellant is an inventory margin, not a guarantee of finite-time reachable acceleration after thrust loss or late retargeting.
